items.title: What are the main events for today?
items.summary: EUROPEAN SESSION In the European session, the only highlight is the Swiss CPI which is expected to decline to 0.5% vs 0.6% prior on a year-over-year basis. The data is not going to change anything for the SNB as the central bank continues to reiterate its neutral stance and willingness to intervene in the FX market if necessary. AMERICAN SESSION In the American session, we get the US NFP report and the US Jobless Claims figures, but the focus will of course be on the NFP. Initial Claims are expected at 218K vs 215K prior, while Continuing Claims are seen at 1820K vs 1821K prior. Jobless Claims have been pointing to a stable/improving labour market until the recent weeks when we started to see some minor easing. Keep in mind that Jobless Claims tend to increase in the first part of the summer due to seasonal factors. The US NFP is expected to show 110K jobs added in June vs 172K in May, and the Unemployment Rate to remain unchanged at 4.3%. The Average Hourly Earnings Y/Y is expected at 3.5% vs 3.4% prior, while the M/M measure is seen at 0.3% vs 0.3% prior. Data in line or worse than forecasts could trigger a pullback in the markets, with the US dollar likely falling across the board. Upside surprises, especially on the unemployment rate and wage growth side, might lead to some minor hawkish repricing. All in all, I expect the US CPI report to matter more as the Fed's focus has shifted towards inflation. CENTRAL BANK SPEAKERS 11:30 GMT/07:30 ET - ECB's Escriva (neutral - voter) 11:45 GMT/07:45 ET - Fed's Daly (neutral - non voter) 15:45 GMT/11:45 ET - BoE's Mann (neutral - voter) 17:55 GMT/13:55 ET - ECB's Cipollone (neutral - voter) This article was written by Giuseppe Dellamotta at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 欧洲时段 在欧洲时段，唯一的焦点是瑞士CPI数据，预计同比增速将从前值0.6%降至0.5%。该数据不会对瑞士央行（SNB）的政策产生任何影响，因为该央行继续重申其中立立场，并表示必要时愿意干预外汇市场。美股时段 在美股时段，我们将迎来美国非农就业报告和初请失业金人数数据，但市场焦点自然集中在非农就业报告上。初请失业金人数预计为21.8万，前值为21.5万；续请失业金人数预计为182万，前值为182.1万。失业救济金申请数据此前一直显示劳动力市场稳定且有所改善，直到最近几周才开始出现小幅回落。需注意的是，受季节性因素影响，失业救济金申请数量在夏季前半段往往会上升。预计6月美国非农就业人数将增加11万，低于5月的17.2万，失业率将维持在4.3%不变。预计平均时薪同比增速为3.5%，高于此前3.4%的水平；环比增速预计为0.3%，与此前持平。 若数据符合或低于预期，可能引发市场回调，美元可能全面走低。若数据好于预期——尤其是失业率和工资增长方面——可能会导致市场出现小幅的鹰派重新定价。总而言之，鉴于美联储的关注重点已转向通胀，我预计美国CPI报告将更为重要。央行官员讲话 格林尼治时间11:30/美国东部时间07:30 - 欧洲央行埃斯克里瓦（中性立场 - 有投票权） 格林尼治时间11:45/美国东部时间07:45 - 美联储的戴利（中性——无投票权） 格林尼治时间15:45/东部时间11:45 - 英格兰银行的曼恩（中性——有投票权） 格林尼治时间17:55/东部时间13:55 - 欧洲央行的奇波洛内（中性——有投票权） 本文由investinglive.com的朱塞佩·德拉莫塔撰写。

items.title: investingLive Asia-Pacific FX news wrap: Tech sell off slams Kospi, Nikkei lower too
items.summary: More on this: Trump earned $1.4bn from crypto in 2026 as most token holders sit at a loss US payrolls preview: June NFP seen slowing sharply to 110k after May's surge Bank of Korea official says Bank has capacity to take steps on the weak won Australia's trade blows out to big deficit vs. surplus expected Foreigner investors pull record $137bn from Asia stocks as AI rally forces rebalancing PBOC sets USD/ CNY reference rate for today at 6.8088 (vs. estimate at 6.7929) Equites getting hit. South Korea Kospi dumped 6%. Japan's Nikkei down more than 2.5% JPMorgan sees dot-com era warning as AI hardware stocks diverge from spenders Korea inflation hits 2.5-year high as Goldman flags chip stock outflow risk UBS stays bullish on stocks after best quarter in six years, sees rally broadening Apple talks to buy memory chips from Pentagon blacklisted Chinese suppliers. Cook cooked? New Zealand building permits fall in May, down 4% m/m (prior +10.9) Recap: US, Iran end Doha talks with no breakthrough as Hormuz status stays unclear Equity market bargains grow scarce as AI boom lifts stocks to fresh highs MUFG sees euro rallying to 1.20 against dollar by 2027 as ECB nears rate peak Warsh, Lagarde and Bailey signal joint retreat from forward guidance at Sintra Preview: RBNZ tipped to hike 25bp in July as oil slide clouds tightening outlook investingLive Americas FX news wrap 1 Jul: Central bankers worry about inflation US stocks close lower. Midday rise in the Nasdaq fizzles Session Wrap Asia-Pac equities sold off heavily, led by a violent tech rotation out of AI-linked names after Meta's sudden capex discipline rattled Wall Street overnight South Korea bore the brunt, with the KOSPI slumping as much as 6% and triggering a sidecar as Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix each shed more than 7% Japan's Nikkei fell around 1%, softer than the regional selloff, while Hong Kong bucked the trend on local tech and biopharma strength as Shanghai stayed in the red Oil extended its grind lower as US-Iran talks in Doha wrapped with Qatar citing positive progress, though no breakthrough Gold pushed back above $4,000/oz, supported by softer jobs data and falling oil, with all eyes now on today's payrolls print for Fed cues Iron ore briefly topped $100/ton after China's state-backed buyer moved to restrict some Fortescue port inventories, though AUD slipped Apple in talks to source memory chips from blacklisted Chinese suppliers CXMT and YMTC, while Nikkei reports the company is planning five new iPhone models across H2 2026 and H1 2027 Regional equities copped a heavy hit through the session as a sharp rotation out of AI infrastructure names spilled across Asia-Pac markets. The catalyst traced back to Wall Street, where Meta's abrupt signal of capex discipline, reportedly including plans to sell off computing power, reignited concerns about overbuilt AI capacity and triggered a violent selloff in US tech that carried straight through into Asian trade. South Korea felt it hardest. The KOSPI slumped as much as 6% and tripped a sidecar circuit breaker in early trade, with Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix both losing more than 7% and erasing billions in combined market value as the broad US semiconductor rout hit chipmakers directly. Japan's Nikkei was comparatively more contained, down around 1% and off its worst levels of the session, though still weighed by the same tech selling alongside recent upside in yields. Greater China was mixed, with the mainland conforming to the risk-off tone while Hong Kong bucked the trend, supported by strength in local tech, biopharmaceutical and auto names on its return from holiday closure. US equity futures were sitting mildly positive into the local close. Elsewhere, oil continued its slow drift lower following the latest round of indirect US-Iran talks in Doha. Qatar's Foreign Ministry said mediators had concluded separate meetings with both sides and noted positive progress on issues tied to the Islamabad MoU, though no lasting breakthrough was signalled. Gold caught a bid on the back of softer than expected (US) jobs data Wednesday and the retreat in oil, with spot pushing back above the $4,000/oz mark and touching its highest level since June 23 in the prior session before today's payrolls report takes over as the next major catalyst for Fed pricing. Worth flagging for the medium term: a recent OMFIF survey found central banks themselves see gold trading in a $5,000 to $6,000/oz range in twelve months. In commodities, iron ore futures briefly cleared $100 a ton after China's state-backed buyer signalled plans to restrict some Fortescue inventories held at mainland ports. The Aussie slipped back. On the corporate side, Apple is reportedly in active talks to source memory chips from ChangXin Memory Technologies and Yangtze Memory Technologies, both on the Pentagon's Section 1260H blacklist, with any supply earmarked primarily for the Chinese market and no agreement yet finalised. Separately, Nikkei reports Apple is planning a five model iPhone lineup split across H2 2026 and H1 2027, including a larger volume of folding handsets than previously expected. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 更多相关内容：2026年特朗普从加密货币中获利14亿美元，而大多数代币持有者仍处于亏损状态 美国非农就业数据前瞻： 5月就业数据激增后，6月非农就业人数预计将大幅放缓至11万 韩国央行官员表示，央行有能力针对韩元走弱采取措施 澳大利亚贸易收支出现巨额赤字，与预期盈余形成反差 受AI行情推动，外国投资者从亚洲股市撤资创纪录的1370亿美元，迫使投资组合再平衡 中国人民银行将今日美元兑人民币中间价定为6.8088（市场预期为6.7929） 股市承压。韩国KOSPI指数暴跌6%。日本日经指数下跌逾2.5%。摩根大通认为AI硬件股与消费类股走势分化，预示着“互联网泡沫时代”的警钟。韩国通胀率创2.5年来新高，高盛警示芯片股资金流出风险。瑞银在创下六年最佳季度业绩后仍看多股市，预计涨势将进一步扩大。苹果正商谈从五角大楼黑名单上的中国供应商处采购内存芯片。 “Cook”出局？新西兰5月建筑许可量环比下降4%（前值+10.9） 回顾： 美国与伊朗多哈谈判无果而终，霍尔木兹海峡局势仍不明朗 随着AI热潮推动股市创下新高，股市中的超值股日益稀缺 三菱日联金融集团（MUFG）预计，随着欧洲央行利率接近峰值，到2027年欧元兑美元将升至1.20 沃什、拉加德和贝利在辛特拉会议上暗示将共同放弃前瞻性指引 预览： 市场预计新西兰央行将于7月加息25个基点，但油价下跌令紧缩前景蒙上阴影 investingLive美洲外汇新闻综述 7月1日：央行行长们担忧通胀，美股收低。 纳斯达克午盘涨势戛然而止 盘后综述 亚太股市大幅下挫，主要受科技股剧烈轮动拖累——此前Meta突然收紧资本支出计划，令隔夜华尔街市场震动。韩国股市受冲击最重，KOSPI指数一度暴跌6%，触发熔断机制，其中三星电子和SK海力士股价均下跌逾7% 日本日经指数下跌约1%，跌幅小于该地区整体抛售幅度；香港市场则逆势上扬，得益于本地科技股和生物制药股的强劲表现，而上海股市仍处于下跌状态。原油价格延续跌势，尽管卡塔尔表示多哈举行的美伊谈判取得了积极进展，但未取得突破。黄金价格重新回升至每盎司4,000美元以上， 这得益于疲软的就业数据和油价下跌，市场目光现正聚焦于今日的非农就业报告以寻找美联储政策信号；受中国国有买家采取措施限制部分Fortescue港口库存影响，铁矿石价格一度突破每吨100美元， 但澳元汇率仍出现下跌。苹果公司正与被列入黑名单的中国供应商CXMT和YMTC商谈采购内存芯片事宜，而《日经新闻》报道称，该公司计划在2026年下半年至2027年上半年推出五款新款iPhone。受AI基础设施板块资金大幅撤离影响，区域股市在整个交易时段遭受重创 亚太市场股指全线下跌。这一走势的导火索源自华尔街：据报道，Meta突然释放出将严格控制资本支出的信号，其中包括出售计算能力的计划，这重新点燃了市场对人工智能产能过剩的担忧，并引发了美国科技股的剧烈抛售，这一跌势直接蔓延至亚洲交易时段。韩国市场受冲击最为严重。韩国KOSPI指数早盘一度暴跌6%，触发“侧车”熔断机制；受美国半导体板块全面暴跌的直接冲击，三星电子和SK海力士双双下跌逾7%，合计蒸发了数十亿市值。相比之下，日本日经指数跌幅相对有限，下跌约1%，且已从盘中低点反弹，但仍受同一波科技股抛售潮以及近期收益率上升的拖累。大中华区股市涨跌不一：内地股市顺应了避险情绪，而香港股市则逆势上扬，得益于假期休市后本地科技、生物制药和汽车类股的强劲表现。美国股指期货在当地收盘前小幅走高。此外，在多哈举行新一轮美伊间接谈判后，油价继续缓慢走低。 卡塔尔外交部表示，调解员已分别与双方举行了会谈，并指出在与《伊斯兰堡谅解备忘录》相关的问题上取得了积极进展，尽管尚未出现持久的突破。受周三（美国）就业数据弱于预期以及油价回落的提振，黄金买盘涌现，现货金价重新回升至每盎司4,000美元上方，并在今日非农就业报告发布前一交易日触及6月23日以来的最高水平——该报告将成为美联储政策定价的下一个主要催化剂。值得关注的中期因素：OMFIF最近的一项调查显示，各国央行自身预计未来12个月黄金价格将在每盎司5,000至6,000美元区间内波动。在大宗商品方面，中国国有买家暗示计划限制部分存放在内地港口的福特斯克矿业库存后，铁矿石期货价格一度突破每吨100美元。澳元汇率随之回落。企业方面，据报道，苹果公司正在积极与长信存储和长江存储进行谈判，计划从这两家均被列入五角大楼第1260H条黑名单的企业采购内存芯片，相关供应主要面向中国市场，但目前尚未达成最终协议。 此外，《日经新闻》报道称，苹果计划推出五款iPhone机型，发布时间将分在2026年下半年和2027年上半年，其中折叠屏手机的产量将高于此前预期。本文作者为investinglive.com的Eamonn Sheridan。

items.title: investingLive Asia-Pacific FX news wrap: Asia-Pac equities claw back early losses
items.summary: UBS sees Fed on hold as Warsh downplays inflation risk despite hike bets China services growth eases slightly as export orders hit 20-month high Japan fin min says ready to respond to FX moves PBOC sets USD/ CNY reference rate for today at 6.8047 (vs. estimate at 6.7808) Canada - PM Carney confirms southern Trans Mountain route for new Alberta pipeline Japan services activity rebounds as input costs hit four-year inflation high Investors demand $15.6 billion from private credit, get back just $5.9 billion. Australian services activity returns to growth but confidence hits 2.5-year low NZ consumer confidence rebounds as inflation expectations reset lower Lagarde defends June rate hike as core inflation and supply shock persist Saudi Hormuz oil shipments surge as post-war tanker backlog clearing (still below pre-war) UBS slash oil price forecasts sharply, Q3 down $25. Oil recap - Doha encouragement. US stocks close the holiday shortened week mixed investingLive Americas market news wrap: US dollar slides as non-farm payrolls miss Summary points: Asia-Pac equities recovered from an early sell-off, with the Nikkei and KOSPI leading a broad-based bounce back into positive territory Japan's services PMI rose to 52.2 in June from 50.0, with a stronger yen and softer oil prices driving rotation out of tech into cyclicals and consumer names Gold jumped over 1% as soft US jobs data pared back expectations for further Fed tightening USD/JPY held around 161.00-161.50 in subdued trade, with fresh verbal intervention from Japan's finance minister keeping traders cautious ahead of the thin-liquidity US holiday Canada's Carney unveiled a $200 billion pipeline and Pathways carbon capture package, with Trans Mountain and Pembina building a new VLCC-capable export route to the Pacific China's PBOC trimmed government bond purchases to a nine-month low in June, signalling growing caution over further yield declines Asia-Pacific equities staged a solid recovery on Friday, bouncing back from an early sell-off to finish broadly higher across the region. Japan's Nikkei 225 was choppy through the session, sliding as much as 1.6% before clawing back the losses to trade up 0.7% by lunch, while the broader Topix climbed 0.9%. The volatility reflected a tug-of-war between lingering concerns over stretched tech valuations and a run of encouraging domestic data, with the KOSPI following a similar path to also finish in the green. Underpinning the recovery was a stronger-than-expected reading on Japan's service sector. The final S&P Global Japan Services PMI for June rose to 52.2 from a neutral 50.0 in May, reinforcing the sense that domestic demand remains a source of resilience even as export orders soften. A firmer yen and falling oil prices helped trigger a rotation out of technology names and into cyclical and consumer-facing sectors, a shift that traders will be watching closely heading into next week for signs of durability. Gold was the standout mover elsewhere, rising more than 1% after Thursday's softer-than-expected US jobs data tempered expectations for further Federal Reserve tightening. Both the official nonfarm payrolls print and the preceding ADP release came in weaker than forecast, easing concerns around a higher-for-longer rate path and giving bullion a clear tailwind. Oil futures were far more muted by comparison, eking out only mild gains in rangebound trade with no fresh geopolitical catalysts to drive direction. Currency markets stayed largely subdued, though USD/JPY saw somewhat busier two-way trade around the 161.00 to 161.50 area. Verbal intervention from Japan's finance minister added a layer of caution for traders, a reminder that Tokyo remains alert to disorderly yen moves even as broader FX ranges stayed tight heading into the thinly traded US holiday session. On the geopolitical and infrastructure front, Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney used a press conference to unveil the terms of a long-awaited West Coast pipeline deal. Trans Mountain, the government-owned pipeline operator, has been tapped to build a new export line connecting Alberta's oil sands to a Vancouver-area port capable of handling very large crude carriers, with construction potentially starting as early as September 2027. Pembina Pipeline has come on as a non-binding co-proponent alongside Trans Mountain, Alberta and the federal government, while a tripartite agreement with oil sands companies pairs investment incentives with regulatory reform to help ensure the pipeline runs at capacity. Carney put the combined value of the associated projects, including the Pathways carbon capture initiative, at around $200 billion. Rounding out the session, data from China showed the People's Bank of China trimmed its government bond purchases to their smallest scale in nine months in June. The pullback suggests policymakers are growing more cautious about the risk of further yield declines or excess liquidity building in the system, a subtle but notable shift in tone from Beijing heading into the third quarter. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 瑞银预计美联储将按兵不动，尽管市场押注加息，但沃什淡化了通胀风险；中国服务业增长略有放缓，出口订单创20个月新高；日本财务大臣表示已做好应对汇率波动的准备；中国人民银行将今日美元兑人民币中间价定为6.8047（市场预期为6.7808） 加拿大——总理卡尼确认阿尔伯塔省新输油管道将采用跨山管道南部路线 日本服务业活动反弹，投入成本通胀率创四年新高 投资者寻求156亿美元私募信贷，仅获59亿美元。澳大利亚服务业活动重回增长轨道，但信心指数跌至2.5年低点；新西兰消费者信心回升，通胀预期下调；拉加德在核心通胀和供应冲击持续的情况下为6月加息辩护；随着战后油轮积压订单逐步消化，沙特经霍尔木兹海峡的原油运输量激增（仍低于战前水平）；瑞银大幅下调油价预测，第三季度油价下调25美元。油市回顾——多哈会议带来积极信号。 美国股市在假期缩短的一周中收盘涨跌不一 investingLive 美洲市场新闻综述：非农就业数据不及预期，美元走低 要点总结： 亚太股市从早盘抛售中反弹，日经指数和韩国KOSPI指数领涨，带动大盘全面回升至正值区域。日本6月服务业PMI从50.0升至52.2，日元走强和油价回落推动资金从科技股转向周期股和消费类股。黄金价格上涨逾1% ，因疲软的美国就业数据削弱了市场对美联储进一步收紧政策的预期。美元兑日元在交投清淡中维持在161.00-161.50区间，日本财务大臣的新一轮口头干预使交易员在流动性较薄弱的美国假期前保持谨慎。加拿大央行行长卡尼公布了一项2000亿美元的管道及“路径”碳捕获计划， 其中Trans Mountain和Pembina公司将建设一条通往太平洋、可容纳超大型油轮（VLCC）的新出口路线。中国央行6月将国债购买规模缩减至九个月低点，表明其对收益率进一步下行持日益谨慎的态度。亚太股市周五强劲反弹，从早盘的抛售中回升，该地区各市场普遍收高。 日本日经225指数全天走势震荡，盘中一度下跌1.6%，随后收复失地，午盘时上涨0.7%；而涵盖范围更广的东证指数则上涨0.9%。这种波动反映了市场对科技股估值过高这一持续担忧，与一系列令人鼓舞的国内经济数据之间的拉锯战，韩国KOSPI指数走势与之类似，最终也收涨。支撑市场反弹的是日本服务业数据好于预期。标普全球6月日本服务业PMI终值从5月的中性水平50.0升至52.2，这进一步印证了即便出口订单有所放缓，国内需求仍是经济韧性的来源。日元走强和油价下跌促使资金从科技股流出，转向周期性行业和消费类板块；随着下周临近，交易员将密切关注这一转变，以判断其可持续性。黄金是其他市场中表现最为突出的品种，周四美国就业数据弱于预期，削弱了市场对美联储进一步收紧政策的预期，金价因此上涨逾1%。 无论是官方公布的非农就业数据，还是此前发布的ADP就业报告，实际表现均弱于预期，这缓解了市场对“利率更高且维持更久”路径的担忧，并为黄金价格提供了明显的利好支撑。相比之下，原油期货走势则平淡得多，在区间震荡中仅小幅上涨，因缺乏新的地缘政治催化剂来推动价格走势。外汇市场整体保持沉寂，不过美元兑日元在161.00至161.50区间内出现了较为活跃的双向交易。日本财务大臣的口头干预让交易员们多了一分谨慎，这提醒市场，尽管在交易清淡的美国假期交易时段来临之际，外汇市场整体波动幅度仍保持在较低水平，但东京方面仍对日元无序波动保持警惕。在地缘政治和基础设施方面，加拿大总理马克·卡尼在新闻发布会上公布了备受期待的西海岸输油管道协议的具体条款。政府控股的输油管道运营商Trans Mountain已被选中，负责建设一条连接阿尔伯塔省油砂区与温哥华地区港口的新出口管道，该港口具备接纳超大型原油运输船的能力，工程最早可能于2027年9月动工。 彭比纳管道公司（Pembina Pipeline）已作为非约束性联合提案方加入，与跨山管道公司（Trans Mountain）、阿尔伯塔省及联邦政府共同推进该项目；与此同时，与油砂企业达成的三方协议将投资激励措施与监管改革相结合，以确保该管道满负荷运行。卡尼估计，包括“路径”（Pathways）碳捕获计划在内的相关项目总价值约为2000亿加元。本场会议的最后，来自中国的数据显示，中国人民银行6月份将政府债券购买规模缩减至九个月以来的最低水平。这一收紧举措表明，政策制定者对收益率进一步下跌或系统内流动性过剩的风险日益谨慎，这标志着北京方面在进入第三季度之际，政策基调发生了微妙但显著的转变。本文作者为investinglive.com的伊蒙·谢里丹（Eamonn Sheridan）。
items.summaryEn: UBS sees Fed on hold as Warsh downplays inflation risk despite hike bets China services growth eases slightly as export orders hit 20-month high Japan fin min says ready to respond to FX moves PBOC sets USD/ CNY reference rate for today at 6.8047 (vs. estimate at 6.7808) Canada - PM Carney confirms southern Trans Mountain route for new Alberta pipeline Japan services activity rebounds as input costs hit four-year inflation high Investors demand $15.6 billion from private credit, get back just $5.9 billion. Australian services activity returns to growth but confidence hits 2.5-year low NZ consumer confidence rebounds as inflation expectations reset lower Lagarde defends June rate hike as core inflation and supply shock persist Saudi Hormuz oil shipments surge as post-war tanker backlog clearing (still below pre-war) UBS slash oil price forecasts sharply, Q3 down $25. Oil recap - Doha encouragement. US stocks close the holiday shortened week mixed investingLive Americas market news wrap: US dollar slides as non-farm payrolls miss Summary points: Asia-Pac equities recovered from an early sell-off, with the Nikkei and KOSPI leading a broad-based bounce back into positive territory Japan's services PMI rose to 52.2 in June from 50.0, with a stronger yen and softer oil prices driving rotation out of tech into cyclicals and consumer names Gold jumped over 1% as soft US jobs data pared back expectations for further Fed tightening USD/JPY held around 161.00-161.50 in subdued trade, with fresh verbal intervention from Japan's finance minister keeping traders cautious ahead of the thin-liquidity US holiday Canada's Carney unveiled a $200 billion pipeline and Pathways carbon capture package, with Trans Mountain and Pembina building a new VLCC-capable export route to the Pacific China's PBOC trimmed government bond purchases to a nine-month low in June, signalling growing caution over further yield declines Asia-Pacific equities staged a solid recovery on Friday, bouncing back from an early sell-off to finish broadly higher across the region. Japan's Nikkei 225 was choppy through the session, sliding as much as 1.6% before clawing back the losses to trade up 0.7% by lunch, while the broader Topix climbed 0.9%. The volatility reflected a tug-of-war between lingering concerns over stretched tech valuations and a run of encouraging domestic data, with the KOSPI following a similar path to also finish in the green. Underpinning the recovery was a stronger-than-expected reading on Japan's service sector. The final S&P Global Japan Services PMI for June rose to 52.2 from a neutral 50.0 in May, reinforcing the sense that domestic demand remains a source of resilience even as export orders soften. A firmer yen and falling oil prices helped trigger a rotation out of technology names and into cyclical and consumer-facing sectors, a shift that traders will be watching closely heading into next week for signs of durability. Gold was the standout mover elsewhere, rising more than 1% after Thursday's softer-than-expected US jobs data tempered expectations for further Federal Reserve tightening. Both the official nonfarm payrolls print and the preceding ADP release came in weaker than forecast, easing concerns around a higher-for-longer rate path and giving bullion a clear tailwind. Oil futures were far more muted by comparison, eking out only mild gains in rangebound trade with no fresh geopolitical catalysts to drive direction. Currency markets stayed largely subdued, though USD/JPY saw somewhat busier two-way trade around the 161.00 to 161.50 area. Verbal intervention from Japan's finance minister added a layer of caution for traders, a reminder that Tokyo remains alert to disorderly yen moves even as broader FX ranges stayed tight heading into the thinly traded US holiday session. On the geopolitical and infrastructure front, Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney used a press conference to unveil the terms of a long-awaited West Coast pipeline deal. Trans Mountain, the government-owned pipeline operator, has been tapped to build a new export line connecting Alberta's oil sands to a Vancouver-area port capable of handling very large crude carriers, with construction potentially starting as early as September 2027. Pembina Pipeline has come on as a non-binding co-proponent alongside Trans Mountain, Alberta and the federal government, while a tripartite agreement with oil sands companies pairs investment incentives with regulatory reform to help ensure the pipeline runs at capacity. Carney put the combined value of the associated projects, including the Pathways carbon capture initiative, at around $200 billion. Rounding out the session, data from China showed the People's Bank of China trimmed its government bond purchases to their smallest scale in nine months in June. The pullback suggests policymakers are growing more cautious about the risk of further yield declines or excess liquidity building in the system, a subtle but notable shift in tone from Beijing heading into the third quarter. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.
